title: Telemedicine | Aurora Derm
description: Remote dermatology with clear first guidance, careful diagnosis, a responsible in-clinic handoff, and follow-up in Quito.
heading: Dermatology telemedicine in Quito

heroImage.alt: Telemedicine intake with clinical context and imaging

lead: Teledermatology works best when early remote guidance clarifies whether to keep moving online or switch into clinic.

thesis.title: Teledermatology to start well, switch on time, and know next steps
thesis.body: It clarifies when remote care helps, when it is no longer enough, and which next action keeps the case from staying half-decided.

statement.title: Teledermatology with clear limits, an in-clinic handoff, and a visible next action
statement.alt: Remote dermatology review with follow-up and adjustment

internalMessage.description: Remote guidance works when there is a good history, useful photos, and a concrete question; if warning signs or meaningful uncertainty appear, we move quickly into clinic.

blocks.title: When it does help
items: Known flares that need adjustment without waiting for a full clinic visit.
Questions between reviews when progression can be compared through photos and history.
Follow-up after a recent treatment or routine change.
Early orientation when you need to know whether arranging clinic soon makes sense.

blocks.title: When it is not enough
items: Blurred images or symptoms that still lack enough context.
New lesions with rapid change that have not been examined before.
Intense pain, bleeding, drainage, or functional impact.
Likely need for dermatoscopy, procedure, or direct physical examination.

blocks.title: Move into clinic and next action
items: When the first remote read still leaves clinical doubt that should not be dragged out.
When the first plan does not respond as expected in the agreed review window.
When the case needs dermatoscopy, a procedure, or direct physical examination.
When the correct next step is already to open first visit instead of waiting longer online.

initiatives.title: Prepare useful photos
initiatives.copy: Natural light, steady distance, and one context photo make the first read much clearer.

initiatives.title: Explain what changed first
initiatives.copy: Timing, symptoms, and previous treatment help decide whether this remote start is enough.

initiatives.title: Watch for switching signals
initiatives.copy: Pain, bleeding, fast spread, or poor response are signs to move from remote care into clinic without stretching the wait.

initiatives.title: Open first visit
initiatives.copy: When the decision already requires direct examination, the correct action is to open first visit instead of staying remote.

bookingStatus.title: Online booking under maintenance
bookingStatus.description: If this remote review shows that direct examination matters, the next step is to open first visit in Quito.
